---
title: "Diversity by Neighbourhood"
description: "Visible-minority groups across Canada's cities, by census tract — 2021 Census"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_groups_map
```

The share of each visible-minority group in every **census tract** — roughly a
neighbourhood of 2,500–8,000 people — from the 2021 Census. **Use the dropdown to switch
between groups, and zoom and pan to your own area.** Census tracts cover cities and larger
towns; sparsely populated rural areas aren't divided into tracts, so they appear blank.

This is a descriptive picture of residential geography — "visible minority" is Statistics
Canada's term, and it is a separate concept from Indigenous identity. There is no "good" or
"bad" reading here; it simply shows where people live. The **"Not a visible minority"** option
is StatCan's residual category — it includes white *and* Indigenous people; the census has no
distinct "White" group.

This detailed view lives on its own page (≈3 MB of boundary data) so the main
[Population](index.qmd) page stays fast to load.

[← Back to Population](index.qmd)

```{python}
#| echo: false
geo_path = "data/geo/ct_2021.geojson"
eth_path = "data/geo/statcan_ct_ethnicity_2021.csv"
if os.path.exists(geo_path) and os.path.exists(eth_path):
    ct_geo = json.load(open(geo_path))
    ct_df = pd.read_csv(eth_path, dtype={"ctuid": str})
    groups = [("all_vm", "All visible minorities"), ("south_asian", "South Asian"),
              ("chinese", "Chinese"), ("black", "Black"), ("filipino", "Filipino"),
              ("arab", "Arab"), ("latin_american", "Latin American"),
              ("southeast_asian", "Southeast Asian"), ("west_asian", "West Asian"),
              ("korean", "Korean"), ("japanese", "Japanese"),
              ("not_vm", "Not a visible minority")]
    fig = choropleth_groups_map(ct_geo, ct_df, "ctuid", groups, name_col="name",
        colorscale="Purples", height=720,
        source_note="Source: 2021 Census (Statistics Canada, Census Profile 98-401-X2021007); % of population by census tract. 'Visible minority' is StatCan's term; 'Not a visible minority' includes white and Indigenous people. Descriptive only.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Census-tract diversity map not available — run `python -m pipeline.build_census_geo`.")
```

[← Back to Population](index.qmd)
